channel.title: Awesome Search KG
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Awesome Search KG

item.title: Doug Turnbull
item.description: Doug Turnbull Co-founder of OpenSource Connections (OSC); co-author of Relevant Search (O’Reilly).

item.title: Adopting Vespa for Recommendation Retrieval at Vinted
item.description: Adopting Vespa for Recommendation Retrieval at Vinted Vinted (Europe’s largest second-hand fashion marketplace) migrated from FAISS to Vespa for serving personalized homepage recommendations.

item.title: Agentic Search for Context Engineering
item.description: Agentic Search for Context Engineering Long-form write-up of the workshop given at AI Engineer Europe 2026 (London, April 8, 2026).

item.title: Beyond Semantic Similarity: Rethinking Retrieval for Agentic Search via Direct Corpus Interaction
item.description: Beyond Semantic Similarity: Rethinking Retrieval for Agentic Search via Direct Corpus Interaction Authors: TIGER-Lab (Zhuofeng Li et al.) Abstract Modern retrieval systems — whether lexical or semantic — expose a corpus through a fixed similarity interface compressing access into a single top-k retr...

item.title: Building Search-Based Apps With Typesense: From Zero to Hero
item.description: Building Search-Based Apps With Typesense: From Zero to Hero Author: Paul Karikari Source: medium.com/@paulkarikari/building-search-based-apps-with-typesense-from-zero-to-hero-79f84d142aed Summary A hands-on tutorial walking through building a search-based application using Typesense — an open-sourc...

item.title: Building Smarter Search Products - 3 Steps for Evaluating Search Algorithms
item.description: Building Smarter Search Products: 3 Steps for Evaluating Search Algorithms Shopify describes their three-step framework for evaluating new search algorithms (specifically: Query-specific Pagerank vs.

item.title: Choosing a Vector Database for ANN Search at Reddit
item.description: Choosing a Vector Database for ANN Search at Reddit Reddit’s 2025 engineering evaluation of vector databases for ANN search, concluding with the selection of Milvus over Qdrant and others after rigorous qualitative and quantitative testing at Reddit-scale workloads (~340M vectors).

item.title: ColBERT Comes to Apache Solr: Implementation and Tutorial of Late Interaction Model Reranking
item.description: ColBERT Comes to Apache Solr: Implementation and Tutorial of Late Interaction Model Reranking Author: Nicolò Rinaldi (Sease) Summary Tutorial and implementation guide for adding ColBERT-style late interaction reranking to Apache Solr.

item.title: ColBERT-Zero: To Pre-train Or Not To Pre-train ColBERT Models?
item.description: ColBERT-Zero: To Pre-train Or Not To Pre-train ColBERT Models? Authors: Antoine Chaffin, Luca Arnaboldi, Amélie Chatelain, Florent Krzakala (LightOn AI / EPFL) Summary State-of-the-art ColBERT models are conventionally built by taking a strong dense model and adding a small Knowledge Distillation st...

item.title: Common Pitfalls of Onsite Search Experimentation Part 2
item.description: Common Pitfalls of Onsite Search Experimentation Part 2 Siegfried Schüle (searchHub) exposes the basket attribution trap in search A/B testing.
